MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-23-2016 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF MARY NICHOLS' MOTION TO QUASH DEPOSITION SUBPOENAS FOR PRODUCTION OF BUSINESS RECORDS - MOTION TO QUASH DENIED. WHILE THE LANGUAGE OF THE SUBPOENAS IN THE ABSTRACT COULD BE OVERBROAD, IT IS NOT CLEAR IF PLAINTIFF HAD OTHER UNRELATED CONDITIONS FOR WHICH SHE SOUGHT TREATMENT FROM THESE PROVIDERS. (COMPARE BRITT V. SUP. CT. (1978) 20 CAL.3D 844. 864 [PRIVACY APPLIES TO MEDICAL RECORDS] WITH PALAY V. SUP. CT. (1993) 18 CAL.APP.4TH 919, 933-934 [NOT ABSOLUTE RIGHT TO PREVENT DISCOVERY OF RELEVANT MEDICAL RECORDS].) MOREOVER, THERE IS NO PRIVILEGE WHERE THE PATIENTS CONDITION IS AT ISSUE. (EVID. CODE 999; IN RE LIFSCHUTZ (1970) 2 CAL.3D 415, 436-437 [BURDEN ON PLAINTIFF TO SET FORTH NATURE OF CLAIM].) IF PLAINTIFF COUNSEL SEEKS TO CHALLENGE THE TENTATIVE RULING, SHE SHOULD MEET AND CONFER WITH DEFENSE COUNSEL PRIOR TO THE HEARING TO DISCUSS THESE UNRELATED CONDITIONS THAT SHE DOES NOT WISH TO DISCLOSURE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PRO-TEM: ROBERT KANE; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION: DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER PLAINTIFF'S APPLICATION FOR A LOAN MODIFICATION WAS COMPLETE PRIOR TO DEFENDANT'S INITIATION OF FORECLOSURE PROCEEDINGS. DEFENDANT'S ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO COMPLY WITH CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT, RULE 3.1350 SUBDIVISION (B); THE ISSUES SPECIFICALLY STATED IN THE NOTICE OF MOTION ARE NOT REPEATED VERBATIM IN THE SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-23-2018 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jul-10-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Mar-14-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Jason Julian, (415-398-3344) for Defendant/Moving party Wels Fargo Bank, N.A.
Tiffany Norman (415-823-4566) for Plaitiff/Opposing party Mary Nichols.

Argued by Defendant only. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication:

Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. There is a triable issue of material fact as to whether Plaintiff's application for a loan modification was complete prior to Defendant's initiation of foreclosure proceedings.
Defendant's Alternative Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Defendant failed to comply with California Rules of Court, rule 3.1350 subdivision (b); the issues specifically stated in the Notice of Motion are not repeated verbatim in the Separate Statement of Undisputed Material Facts. Order signed in Open Court. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

Plaintiff Mary Nichols Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records I
Off Calendar Nov-23-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Plaintiff Mary Nichols' Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records - Motion to Quash denied. While the language of the subpoenas in the abstract could be overbroad, it is not clear if plaintiff had other unrelated conditions for which she sought treatment from these providers. (Compare Britt v. Sup. Ct. (1978) 20 Cal.3d 844. 864 [privacy applies to medical records] with Palay v. Sup. Ct. (1993) 18 Cal.App.4th 919, 933-934 [not absolute right to prevent discovery of relevant medical records].) Moreover, there is no privilege where the patients condition is at issue. (Evid. Code 999; In re Lifschutz (1970) 2 Cal.3d 415, 436-437 [burden on plaintiff to set forth nature of claim].) If plaintiff counsel seeks to challenge the tentative ruling, she should meet and confer with defense counsel prior to the hearing to discuss these unrelated conditions that she does not wish to disclosure. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Pro-tem: Robert Kane; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/jpt
